pi_items.verified_value: ["Multiparameter", "CO2_Simulator", "PO_Simulator"] - Device simulator types available in OpenICE for corresponding medical devices
pi_items.source: OpenICE documentation (openice.info) - Device-Adapter Configuration Example lists available device types including Multiparameter, CO2_Simulator, and PO_Simulator. These are simulated medical devices used for demonstration purposes.

pi_items.verified_value: Vital Signs - A real demonstration application in OpenICE Supervisor that displays vital sign data from physiological monitors
pi_items.source: OpenICE website (openice.info/vitals.html and openice.info/docs/3_apps.html) - Documents the Vital Signs app as a demo app for viewing vital signs from monitors. Also mentioned as part of HIMSS15 demonstration.

pi_items.verified_value: Path is syntactically valid; actual creation depends on task execution
pi_items.source: Task specification in task.json explicitly requires writing protocol to this file path. Path follows Linux file system conventions.

pi_items.verified_value: 3 is reasonable; verifier gives full 8 pts for window_increase >= 4, partial 4 pts for >= 2
pi_items.source: Verifier.py lines 99-104 define window interaction scoring: if win_increase >= 4 (8 pts) elif win_increase >= 2 (4 pts). Task expects ~5 windows (3 devices + 1 app + 1 detail view), making 3+ a reasonable minimum.

pi_items.verified_value: 60 - Confirmed as exact threshold for task passage
pi_items.source: Verifier.py line 141: 'passed = score >= 60'. Total possible score is 100 points.

pi_items.metadata_value: Task description mentions 'ASA guidelines for moderate sedation'
pi_items.verified_value: ASA Standards for Basic Anesthetic Monitoring (2011 revision, effective July 1, 2011) specify requirements for moderate sedation including: capnography monitoring for exhaled CO2, pulse oximetry for oxygenation, and hemodynamic monitoring
pi_items.source: American Society of Anesthesiologists (asahq.org) - Standards for Basic Anesthetic Monitoring. Also confirmed by NCBI/PMC publications on 2011 ASA capnography mandate for moderate sedation.

pi_items.metadata_value: Task contextualizes as Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist (CRNA) configuring procedural sedation monitoring
pi_items.verified_value: The three required devices align with ASA guidelines: Multiparameter Monitor (hemodynamics/ECG), Capnography/CO2 device (ventilation/EtCO2 monitoring), Pulse Oximeter (oxygenation/SpO2 monitoring)
pi_items.source: ASA Standards for Basic Anesthetic Monitoring (2011) and medical literature on capnography/pulse oximetry requirements for sedation monitoring. Verified devices match ASA specified monitoring parameters.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring a procedural sedation monitoring station in OpenICE by creating three simulated medical devices (Multiparameter Monitor for hemodynamics/ECG, CO2_Simulator for capnography/ventilation, and PO_Simulator for pulse oximeter/oxygenation) and launching the Vital Signs demonstration application. These devices and application are real components of the OpenICE medical device interoperability platform. The task context aligns with ASA Standards for Basic Anesthetic Monitoring (2011), which specify capnography, pulse oximetry, and hemodynamic monitoring as required for moderate sedation. Data is synthetic, generated by OpenICE device simulators. The task requires writing a protocol document to /home/ga/Desktop/sedation_monitoring_protocol.txt with five specific sections. Pass threshold is 60 out of 100 points, with scoring across device creation (32 pts), application launch and window interaction (18 pts), and protocol file validity and content (50 pts).
